fallback_for_note: Used as fallback defaults only when no dedicated engine file exists for the listed engines. If a dedicated file (e.g. unreal-engine-5.json) exists, it always wins unconditionally, regardless of this field. Among multiple files claiming fallback coverage for the same engine, the file with the closest version match takes priority.

keys.effect: Render resolution scale as a percentage (50-100). Does not affect UI resolution.
keys.notes: Some games replace the default UE4 scalability system with a custom scaler or reset this value on launch. Always check docs/GAMES.md or knowledge/games/ for a game-specific override before applying.
Texture quality is bandwidth-bound rather than GPU-compute-bound. Reducing below 2 rarely improves FPS on mid/high-end hardware.

keys.effect: Post-process effects quality including bloom, lens flare, and ambient occlusion: 0=low, 1=medium, 2=high, 3=epic
Texture streaming pool size in MB. Higher values reduce texture pop-in on GPUs with sufficient VRAM.
keys.notes: Scale with available VRAM: ~1000 for 4-6 GB, ~2000 for 8 GB, ~4000 for 12 GB+.
Always disable in-game VSync. Use G-Sync, FreeSync, or driver-level VSync instead to avoid input latency.

keys.effect: Maximum frame rate cap in Hz (0 = unlimited)
keys.notes: Set to monitor refresh rate. Uncapped causes screen tearing, unnecessary GPU heat, and higher power draw.
